name: Vermont Data Privacy Act (H.121)
summary: Vermont's Data Privacy Act provides comprehensive consumer data privacy rights with notably stronger provisions than most US state privacy laws. It includes a private right of action for consumers, data broker registration requirements building on Vermont's existing data broker law, and strong protections against profiling and automated decision-making.
keyRequirements: Consumer rights to access, delete, correct, and port personal data
Right to opt out of sale, targeted advertising, and automated profiling
Private right of action allowing consumers to sue for violations
Data broker registration and transparency requirements
Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing activities
Heightened protections for sensitive data including biometric and health information
penalties: Enforcement by the Vermont Attorney General. Private right of action with statutory damages. Civil penalties for data broker registration violations. No cure period for consumer-initiated enforcement.
